search_queries_used: site:asahq.org standards postanesthesia care sedation recovery record monitoring
candidate_sources_rejected: endoscopy-only sedation details generalized to every procedure
automatic recovery score and discharge determination
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The BSG section was applied only to its endoscopy setting.
Recovery scores and discharge require actual observations and clinician responsibility.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Patients recovering after anesthesia-provider sedation or monitored anesthesia care; BSG evidence is limited to gastrointestinal endoscopy.
setting_applicability: Immediate recovery in a PACU or equivalent monitored area; ward and home follow-up require separate context.
UAE_applicability: International ASA and BSG evidence requires UAE sedation credentialing, monitoring, rescue, discharge, and facility-policy adaptation.
recency_verification: Current ASA 2024 and BSG 2023 sources were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer ASA postanesthesia standard or BSG sedation guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The ASA standard supports a documented recovery record and ongoing evaluation of oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain, nausea, hydration, and other observed domains without auto-populating findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The BSG section supports monitoring until return to baseline and documented recovery only in gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation; it is not generalized to all sedation procedures.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Discharge responsibility and criteria were reviewed, but no discharge decision or criteria are inferred from the generic legacy scaffold.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports qualified discharge responsibility and institution-approved discharge criteria as recorded decisions, not automatic clearance.

unresolved_source_gaps: Procedure, sedation type, medicine, dose, oxygen, airway, respiratory and cardiovascular observations, consciousness, pain, nausea, temperature, events, intervention, response, baseline recovery, score, discharge decision, escort, instructions, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
